top_categories.workout_context: Neusiedlersee Radmarathon's flat and fast with minor rolling sections rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
78 miles on flat and fast with minor rolling sections burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Neusiedlersee Radmarathon's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
When someone attacks on flat and fast with minor rolling sections, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Neusiedlersee Radmarathon. Train it until it feels boring.
Neusiedlersee Radmarathon's flat and fast with minor rolling sections forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 78 miles.
At Neusiedlersee Radmarathon, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
2-minute all-out efforts on flat and fast with minor rolling sections. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Neusiedlersee Radmarathon's flat and fast with minor rolling sections actually does to you.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Neusiedlersee Radmarathon, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Base fitness for Neusiedlersee Radmarathon. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Neusiedlersee Radmarathon’s 78 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, Durability, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 78 miles of flat and fast with minor rolling sections in Mörbisch am See, Burgenland, Austria.
